ccel.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

source.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

title: To Sebastian, Bishop.

source.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Gregory to Sebastian, Bishop of Sirmium.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: I have received the most sweet and pleasant letter of thy Fraternity, which, though you are never absent from my heart, has nevertheless made your Holiness as it were present with me bodily.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But I beseech Almighty God to protect you with His right hand, and to grant you a tranquil life here, and, when it shall please Him, eternal rewards.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But I beg you, if you love me with that love wherewith you always loved me when we were together, to pray for me more earnestly, that so Almighty God may loose me from the bands of my sins, and make me to stand free in His sight, released from the burden of this corruption.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For, however inestimable be the sweetness of the heavenly country for drawing one towards it, yet there are many sorrows in this life to impel us daily to the love of heavenly things.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And these only please me exceedingly from the very fact that they do not allow anything to please me in this world.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For we can by no means describe, most holy brother, what we suffer in this land at the hands of your friend, the lord Romanus.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Yet I may briefly say that his malice towards us has surpassed the swords of the Lombards; so that the enemies who kill us seem kinder than the judges of the republic, who by their malice, rapines, and deceits wear us out with anxiety.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And to bear at the same time the charge of bishops and clergy, and also of monasteries and people, and to watch anxiously against the plots of the enemy, and to be ever suspicious of the deceitfulness and malice of the dukes; what labours and what sorrows all this involves, your Fraternity may the more truly estimate as you more purely love me who suffer these things

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Furthermore, while addressing you with the greeting that I owe you, I inform you that it has come to my knowledge from the report of Boniface the defensor, that our brother the most holy lord Anastasius the patriarch has wished to commit to you the government of the Church in one of his cities, and that you have refused your assent.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: This your feeling and your wisdom I most gladly approve of, and strongly commend; and I account you happy, and myself unhappy in having consented at such a time as this to undertake the government of the Church.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: If, however, by any chance, in condescension to your brethren, and as being intent on works of mercy, you should ever decide to consent to such a proposal, I beg you by no means to prefer any one else’s love to mine.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For there are in the island of Sicily Churches without bishops, and, if by the guidance of God you are pleased to take the government of a Church, you will be able to do this better near the threshold of the blessed apostle Peter, with his aid.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But if you are not so pleased, remain happily as you are, that this resolution may continue in you; and pray for us unhappy ones.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Now may Almighty God keep you under His protection, in whatever place it be His will that you should be, and bring you to heavenly rewards.
